symptoms.symptom_name: Intense localised pruritus dominating over pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: large local reaction to an insect bite
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Itch-predominant inflammation is characteristic of a histaminergic hypersensitivity reaction.
Cellulitis is typically pain-predominant and hot rather than intensely itchy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm indurated wheal with a single central punctum after a bite

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: large local reaction to an insect bite
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A central punctum at the apex of a wheal that appeared immediately after a sharp bite is highly specific for an arthropod bite reaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset over hours (rapid) rather than days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cellulitis characteristically evolves over 1-3 days; an hours-timeline favours a hypersensitivity reaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of systemic allergic features (breathing, lip/tongue/throat, generalised hives, dizziness)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anaphylaxis requires multi-system involvement; a strictly localised reaction with a clear systemic-negative screen excludes it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No lymphangitic streak, afebrile, systemically well

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of spreading erythema, lymphangitis and fever makes established cellulitis unlikely, though early cellulitis cannot be fully excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs (BP 128/80, HR 84, Temp 36.9C, RR 16, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured normal haemodynamics and saturation exclude circulatory/respiratory compromise — sealed because the AI Doctor cannot measure vitals without a device and must infer stability from the patient's reported symptoms.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear pulmonary auscultation and clear oropharynx with no uvular oedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician examination confirming no airway involvement — sealed clinician-only finding not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: 4 cm erythematous indurated wheal on the right lateral calf on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: large local reaction to an insect bite
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpated induration and measured lesion size confirm a large local reaction — the AI Doctor can only approximate this from the video-observable finding offered in the presentation layer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count / peripheral eosinophil count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not acutely indicated for a localised bite reaction; it would only assist in atypical or chronic cases and ordering it here is unnecessary.
There is no evidence of infection; swabbing an uninfected bite is not indicated.
